Empires & Puzzles Voluptas Review: Is This 4-Star Healer Worth It?
Is Voluptas a sweet treat or a stale cake? We dive into the Season of Love's 4-star healer to see if her fiend destruction and localized healing can stand up to the modern meta.
Voluptas Review: The Cupcake Healer That Leaves You Hungry
Voluptas: The "Cupid of Delights" That Serves a Slice of Disappointment

Voluptas is a 4-star Epic Holy (Yellow) hero from the Season of Love event. Released back in February 2023, she arrived with a tray full of pastries and a skill set that left many players scratching their heads. As a Druid, she brings a bit of durability, but her role as a hybrid healer and fiend destroyer is marred by a restricted area of effect. In a world of overhealers and board-wide cleansers, Voluptas often feels like she brought a cupcake to a banquet.

Visually, Voluptas is a delight—pun intended. Dressed in a chef's uniform with angel wings and serving a massive "Flat Cake," she leans fully into the whimsical, romantic theme of the Season of Love. It’s a refreshing break from the usual armored warriors and grim sorcerers. Her expression is pure joy, which is ironic considering the frustration she often causes when you pull her instead of a 5-star event hero. It’s hard to be mad at someone offering you cake, but when that cake only heals three people, the smile fades a bit.

When we look at Voluptas's stats in the context of the current meta (early 2026), the picture is grim. With a base Power of 686, Attack of 650, and Defense of 643, she is significantly behind modern 4-star heroes who regularly push 800+ in stats and boast Power ratings near 800. Even with Limit Breaks, she struggles to compete with the raw bulk and damage output of newer releases like the "Construct" or "Astral" families. She is effectively a "Classic+" hero in terms of stats, which makes her very squishy in today's environment.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Cupid family and passives are actually the saving grace that keeps Voluptas from being complete "feeder food."

for unmatched heroes. This is a potent passive buff that helps your entire team recover faster from other sources, not just her own skill. It encourages you to splash her into diverse teams rather than stacking the family.

This specific immunity gives her a genuine niche in Rush Attack tournaments where Alfrike tanks are prevalent. While her teammates might be mindless zombies, Voluptas can still fire her skill (assuming she has mana) to keep the center alive.

Upon release, Voluptas was widely panned by the community as a "dud." Players compared her unfavorably to existing healers like Wang Yuanji or even classic heroes. She never had a "glory days" period; she was considered underwhelming from day one.

The gap has only widened. With modern 4-stars boasting stats that rival old 5-stars and skills that do three or four things for the whole team, Voluptas feels like a relic. The only thing that hasn't aged is her passive immunity.
She is not viable for serious war teams or standard raid attacks. Her only real home is in
where her passive can counter Alfrike or other silencers, or perhaps in the Tower events where you need deep rosters and specific counters to status ailments.
Since Voluptas is a healer with low base defense, survivability is paramount. She needs to live long enough to cast her skill.
Maximizes her ability to take a hit. Since she needs to be in the thick of the action (likely flank or tank in 4-star tournaments) to heal the most relevant allies, she needs bulk.
Her attack stat remains pitiful, making her tile damage negligible.

Passive +20% healing generation for the team (if she's the only family member) is a solid support effect.
Heals and cleanses fiends for only 3 allies. This is her fatal flaw.
Too slow for a "Nearby" effect. Should be Fast.
Extremely low stats compared to 2024-2026 heroes.
Offers no utility against status ailments (other than Fiends) or enemy buffs.
Her immunity to Mindless Attack allows her to fire when everyone else is shut down.
If you lack 5-star counters, she can serve as a budget option to clear fiends in PvE or specific war hits.
In 4-star Rush, Alfrike or other controllers are common. Voluptas ensures that at least the center of your formation can recover HP and clear fiends if the enemy uses fiend summoners.
Do not pair her with other Cupid family heroes if you want the +20% healing bonus. She works best alone.

If you pull Voluptas early in your game, she is a usable healer, especially if you lack Rigard or Boldtusk. The 40% heal is significant. However,
do not prioritize Limit Breaking her
. Save those resources for healers that cover the whole team. Once you get a healer who targets "All," she will likely retire to your bench.

She is a museum piece for you. Unless you are extremely competitive in 4-star Rush Tournaments and desperately need a Holy healer who resists Silence, there is no reason to level her. She is not worth the Alpha Aethers.
The community was not kind to Voluptas upon her release.
"This guy’s a dud... worse than C-Kashhrek or Sangrior."
"I feel this is only a placeholder to make people think that odds are better than they are."
"Why is this hero even a thing when Wang Yuanji exists?"

One user famously said: "I can’t believe they left her in this state. Such a dud."
The general consensus was that a "Caster and Nearby" healer at Average speed was dead on arrival, especially compared to the powerful 5-stars in the same portal.

Voluptas is a hero that looks sweet but leaves a sour taste. While the concept of a "Cupid Chef" is fun, the mechanics of her special skill are simply too restrictive for modern gameplay. She attempts to do two things (Heal and Destroy Fiends) but fails to do either effectively enough to warrant a spot on a serious team. She is best served as a niche counter-pick in tournaments, or perhaps just as a smiling face in your hero roster museum.
Keep one for the collection, but don't expect her to save the day unless the day involves eating cake.
A detailed review and strategy guide for Voluptas, the 4-star Holy healer from the Season of Love. We analyze her stats, skills, and viability in the current Empires & Puzzles meta.
